lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Let's close with the conclusion. The authors call it "the window we are in." And I think the phrase is carefully chosen.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Because windows close. AI has created the crisis. Crisis creates the opportunity. For the first time in generations, the question "why should humans learn at all" is live — not abstract, not rhetorical. Actually live.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: And that question leads to a deeper answer than job preparation ever could. Education is about what AI cannot provide — authentic self-knowledge, genuine judgment, the capacity to ask "why should I believe this" and "who benefits from me believing it." Wisdom, in the older sense.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: The paper's honest line — this is a better mission than job preparation ever was. The tragedy is that it required an existential threat to get us to ask it.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: The framework they've laid out is grounded in neurobiology, humanistic psychology, creativity research, critical theory, philosophy of science. It offers principles, not prescriptions. They're explicit about that distinction — past reforms codified specific practices that became dated.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Principles can generate many implementations. A school in Santiago will look different from a school in Memphis or Seoul. The framework stays the same. The practice is context.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: The line they borrow from Gramsci to close on — pessimism of the intellect, optimism of the will. We can see clearly how the current system functions and why it resists change. We can also choose to build something better.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: And the humility is explicit. This framework will be wrong about things. That is the point. Open for argument, correction, extension. A living draft.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Which is maybe the right posture for anyone working in the middle of a shift this big. Not claiming certainty. Just — trying something honest, naming what you can, asking for help on what you can't.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: If this series got you thinking, read the paper. Argue with the framework. The authors say explicitly — they want the corrections. That is the invitation.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Thanks for walking through it with us.
